summary: Backend API testing for Zteeel (FastAPI + PostgreSQL + PostGIS). All 21 tests pass after fixing 1 backend bug and correcting test assertions. Customer endpoints require JWT auth (not public as described).

critical.issue: SQL syntax error: ':items::jsonb' mixes SQLAlchemy named param syntax with PostgreSQL cast - asyncpg rejects it. Fixed to 'cast(:items as jsonb)'

minor.issue: All customer endpoints require JWT authentication (Depends(get_current_user)). Problem statement described them as public. This may be intentional design but needs clarification.
Returns status='ok' not 'healthy'. Minor inconsistency with typical health check conventions.

action_items: Clarify if customer browse endpoints (nearby deals, nearby vendors, vendor menu/deals) should be public or require auth - currently all require JWT
The POST /api/vendor/deals schema uses 'type' field (not 'deal_type') and POST /api/vendor/rewards uses 'threshold','reward','color' fields - ensure frontend is using correct field names
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 1282 lines - should be split into routers (auth, vendor, customer, orders, payments)
Multiple raw SQL INSERT statements with JSONB columns using mixed parameter syntax - check all JSONB inserts for same issue (only orders was found broken)
Customer browse APIs require auth - this prevents unauthenticated browsing of deals/vendors which may be a UX issue
seed_data_creation: Registered TEST_customer_zteeel@example.com / testpass123 for customer tests. TEST_register_new@zteeel.com also attempted.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All backend APIs working. Customer endpoints require JWT. Payment is MOCKED (Razorpay placeholder). PostGIS nearby queries confirmed working with Mumbai Bandra coords. Vendor seed accounts: maharaja@zteeel.com/vendor123.
rca_of_issue: POST /api/orders 500 error: SQLAlchemy text() with asyncpg driver cannot use '::' PostgreSQL cast syntax inside named parameters. ':items::jsonb' was interpreted as named param ':items' followed by '::jsonb' cast which fails. Fixed by using standard SQL CAST() function: 'cast(:items as jsonb)'.
